#!/bin/bash

# Generate an initial device-authorization policy based on currently connected USB devices

# Get list of currently connected USB devices
USB_DEVICES=$(lsusb)

# Create a new policy file if it does not already exist
if [ ! -f /etc/usbguard/usbguard.conf ]; then
    touch /etc/usbguard/usbguard.conf
fi

# Add rules to the policy file for each connected USB device
for DEVICE in $USB_DEVICES; do
    echo "allow $DEVICE" >> /etc/usbguard/usbguard.conf
done

# Reload the usbguard service to apply the new policy
systemctl reload usbguard
```
